description: If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.0001, what caveat is documented for Activate?

description: The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.17.0001, what limitation is mentioned for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp.

description: Symptom: BGP sessions reset upon reapplying the overlay template, even when the BGP authentication password remains unchanged. Scenario: This issue occurs when a user configures BGP settings via the REST API and then reapplies the overlay template. The reapplication process triggers a reset of BGP sessions, which should only happen if the password changes. Workaround: To prevent the reset of BGP sessions, remove the configured authentication password before reapplying the overlay template.

description: Symptom: The system has device profile enabled with a role and LLDP/CDP/MAC group match configuration. Port 1: Device profile mode is set to block-until-profile-applied Port 2: No device profile mode is configured. When a device matching the group configuration connects to either port, the corresponding profile is applied because the device profile feature is enabled by default on all ports. On Port 1, traffic is blocked until the profile is applied. On Port 2, traffic continues to forward even if no profile is applied. There is no way to disable profile application on Port 2. Scenario: Adjust the device profile group match criteria so that only devices connected to Port 1 match the configuration. However, if the same device moves to Port 2, the profile is still applied because the group match remains valid. Workaround: A command was provided to disable device profile on ports where it is not required.
